prompt: Marketing keeps inventing promos and I'm done hand-coding each one into checkout, so cart.py needs a proper discount engine. API: cart.add_discount(code, kind, value, priority=100, exclusive=False), plus applied_discounts() returning (code, cents_off) pairs in the order they were applied, and total_cents() actually applying them. Two kinds: 'percent' (integer 1-100, taken off the RUNNING total, rounded half-up to the cent) and 'fixed' (cents off, never more than what's left — the total can hit zero but never go negative). Stacking order is ascending priority number, ties broken by code alphabetically, and each discount sees the total left by the ones before it — order changes the math, that's intentional. Exclusive promos don't stack with anything: if any exclusive discounts are registered, exactly one discount applies — whichever exclusive saves the customer the most against the subtotal; break savings ties by lower priority number, then code. Validation: duplicate codes, unknown kinds, percent outside 1-100 or non-int, fixed < 1 all raise ValueError. Everything the cart does today (item accumulation, receipt, total==subtotal when no discounts) must be untouched. python3 test_cart.py is the contract — leave the test file alone.
